# frozen_string_literal: true

module Spree
  # Spree::OrderMerger is responsible for taking two orders and merging them
  # together by adding the line items from additional orders to the order
  # that the OrderMerger is initialized with.
  #
  # Orders that are merged should be incomplete orders which should belong to
  # the same user. They should also be in the same currency.
  class OrderMerger
    # @!attribute order
    #   @api public
    #   @return [Spree::Order] The order which items wll be merged into.
    attr_accessor :order

    # Create the OrderMerger
    #
    # @api public
    # @param [Spree::Order] order The order which line items will be merged
    # into.
    def initialize(order)
      @order = order
    end

    # Merge a second order in to the order the OrderMerger was initialized with
    #
    # The line items from `other_order` will be merged in to the `order` for
    # this OrderMerger object. If the line items are for the same variant, it
    # will add the quantity of the incoming line item to the existing line item.
    # Otherwise, it will assign the line item to the new order.
    #
    # After the orders have been merged the `other_order` will be destroyed.
    #
    # @example
    #   initial_order = Spree::Order.find(1)
    #   order_to_merge = Spree::Order.find(2)
    #   merger = Spree::OrderMerger.new(initial_order)
    #   merger.merge!(order_to_merge)
    #   # order_to_merge is destroyed, initial order now contains the line items
    #   # of order_to_merge
    #
    # @api public
    # @param [Spree::Order] other_order An order which will be merged in to the
    # order the OrderMerger was initialized with.
    # @param [Spree::User] user Associate the order the user specified. If not
    # specified, the order user association will not be changed.
    # @return [void]
    def merge!(other_order, user = nil)
      if other_order.currency == order.currency
        other_order.line_items.each do |other_order_line_item|
          current_line_item = find_matching_line_item(other_order_line_item)
          handle_merge(current_line_item, other_order_line_item)
        end
      end

      set_user(user)
      if order.valid?
        persist_merge

        # So that the destroy doesn't take out line items which may have been re-assigned
        other_order.line_items.reload
        other_order.destroy
      end
    end

    private

    # Retreive a matching line item from the existing order
    #
    # It will compare line items based on variants, and all line item
    # comparison hooks on the order.
    #
    # @api private
    # @param [Spree::LineItem] other_order_line_item The line item from
    # `other_order` we are attempting to merge in.
    # @return [Spree::LineItem] A matching line item from the order. nil if none
    # exist.
    def find_matching_line_item(other_order_line_item)
      order.line_items.detect do |my_li|
        my_li.variant == other_order_line_item.variant &&
          Spree::Config.line_item_comparison_hooks.all? do |hook|
            order.send(hook, my_li, other_order_line_item.serializable_hash)
          end
      end
    end

    # Associate the user with the order
    #
    # @api private
    # @param [Spree::User] user The user to associate with the order. If nil
    # the order user association will remain the same. If the order is already
    # associated with a user, it will not be changed.
    # @return [void]
    def set_user(user)
      order.associate_user!(user) if !order.user && user
    end

    # Merge the `other_order_line_item` into `current_line_item`
    #
    # If `current_line_item` is nil, the `other_order_line_item` will be
    # re-assigned to the `order`.
    #
    # If the merged line item can not be saved, an error will be added to
    # `order`.
    #
    # @api private
    # @param [Spree::LineItem] current_line_item The line item to be merged
    # into. If nil, the `other_order_line_item` will be re-assigned.
    # @param [Spree::LineItem] other_order_line_item The line item to merge in.
    # @return [void]
    def handle_merge(current_line_item, other_order_line_item)
      if current_line_item
        current_line_item.quantity += other_order_line_item.quantity
        handle_error(current_line_item) unless current_line_item.save
      else
        new_line_item = order.line_items.build(other_order_line_item.attributes.except("id"))
        handle_error(new_line_item) unless new_line_item.save
      end
    end

    # Handle an error from saving the `line_item`
    #
    # This adds errors from the line item to the `errors[:base]` of the order.
    #
    # @api private
    # @param [Spree::LineItem] line_item The line item which could not be saved
    # @return [void]
    def handle_error(line_item)
      order.errors.add(:base, line_item.errors.full_messages)
    end

    # Save the order totals after merge
    #
    # It triggers the order recalculator to ensure that item counts and totals are
    # up to date.
    #
    # @api private
    # @return [void]
    def persist_merge
      order.recalculate
    end
  end
end

I now have a complete picture across all seven steps. Here is the audit.

---

# Order Contract Audit — Solidus

## Step 1 — Orientation

**What it is:** Solidus, a full-stack e-commerce framework for Rails, split into cooperating gems (each a Rails engine), all namespaced under `Spree::` (and the newer `SolidusAdmin::` / `SolidusPromotions::`).

**Gem layout** (repo root):
- `core/` — domain models, services, state machines, config (`Spree::Config`). The heart.
- `api/` — JSON API controllers (`Spree::Api::*`).
- `backend/` — legacy admin UI (`Spree::Admin::*`) + `Spree::BackendConfiguration`.
- `admin/` — new admin UI built on ViewComponents (`SolidusAdmin::*`).
- `promotions/` — current promotions system (`SolidusPromotions::*`).
- `legacy_promotions/` — deprecated promotions (`Spree::Promotion::*`), applied as patches.
- `sample/`, `lib/`, `tasks/` — fixtures, meta-gem, rake tasks.

**The central model:** `Spree::Order` — `core/app/models/spree/order.rb:21`. Doc comment (`:4`): "The customers cart until completed, then acts as permanent record of the transaction." Everything hangs off it: line items, shipments, inventory units, payments, adjustments, returns, promotions.

**Shared foundation it's built on:**
- `Spree::Base` — `core/app/models/spree/base.rb:3` (abstract AR base; includes `Spree::Core::Permalinks`, `Spree::RansackableAttributes`).
- `Spree::Config` — the configuration registry that swaps in every collaborator class (recalculator, contents, shipping, cancellations, merger, stock coordinator, promotions adjuster). The Order delegates almost all behavior through it.

---

## Step 2 — The Order contract and what it pulls in

**Model:** `core/app/models/spree/order.rb:21`.

**Concerns / mixins included (`:26`–`:42`):**
- `::Spree::Config.state_machines.order` (`:26`) → `Spree::Core::StateMachines::Order` at `core/app/models/spree/core/state_machines/order.rb:6`, which on include extends `ClassMethods` (`core/app/models/spree/core/state_machines/order/class_methods.rb:7`) and includes `Spree::StateChangeTracking` (`core/app/models/concerns/spree/state_change_tracking.rb:4` — `after_update` enqueues a state-change job).
- `Spree::Order::Payments` (`:28`) → `core/app/models/spree/order/payments.rb:5` (`process_payments!`, `authorize_payments!`, `capture_payments!`, `unprocessed_payments`; return value drives the `complete` transition).
- `Metadata` (`:29`) → `core/app/models/concerns/spree/metadata.rb:4` (adds `customer_metadata`/`admin_metadata` JSON + validation).
- `extend Spree::DisplayMoney` + `money_methods(...)` (`:42`–`:59`) → generates `display_*` for all the money totals.

**Associations other records hang off it (`:78`–`:131`):**
- Customer: `user`, `bill_address`, `ship_address`, `store`, `created_by`, `approver`, `canceler`.
- Items: `line_items` (`:93`, `dependent: :destroy`), `variants`, `products`.
- Shipping: `shipments` (`:98`, `dependent: :destroy`), `inventory_units` (through shipments), `cartons`.
- Adjustments/promos: `adjustments` (`:107`, polymorphic `adjustable`, autosave), `line_item_adjustments`, `shipment_adjustments`, `all_adjustments` (`:110`, `dependent: :destroy`, by `order_id`).
- Payments: `payments` (`:117`, `dependent: :destroy`), `valid_store_credit_payments`.
- Returns: `return_authorizations` (`:121`), `return_items`, `customer_returns`, `reimbursements`, `refunds`.
- `state_changes` (`:128`, polymorphic `stateful`).
- `accepts_nested_attributes_for` line_items/addresses/payments/shipments (`:133`–`:137`).

**Key contract surface a teammate must know:**
- **Recalculation entry point:** `delegate :recalculate, to: :recalculator` (`:165`); `recalculator` memoizes `Spree::Config.order_recalculator_class` (`:281`). `updater` is the deprecated alias (`:284`).
- **Service accessors:** `contents` (`:310`), `shipping` (`:314`), `cancellations` (`:318`), `merge!` (`:457`) — each instantiates a `Spree::Config.*_class`.
- **Teardown methods:** `empty!` (`:461`, destroys line_items/adjustments/shipments, publishes `:order_emptied`, recalculates), `create_proposed_shipments` (`:504`, raises `CannotRebuildShipments` if completed), `check_shipments_and_restart_checkout` (`:525`), `restart_checkout_flow` (`:536`).
- **Lifecycle callbacks:** `after_cancel` (`:846`: cancels shipments+payments, recalculates, publishes `:order_canceled`), `finalize` (`:758`: locks adjustments, recalculates payment/shipment state, publishes `:order_finalized`), `after_resume` (`:869`).
- **Event bus:** publishes `:order_emptied`, `:order_finalized`, `:order_canceled`, and (via recalculator) `:order_recalculated` — external subscribers depend on these.
- **Custom exceptions:** `Spree::Order::InsufficientStock` (`:31`), `CannotRebuildShipments` (`:40`).

---

## Step 3 — How an order is built and recalculated

**Build / mutate (cart → order):**
1. `Spree::SimpleOrderContents` (`core/app/models/spree/simple_order_contents.rb:4`, the `order_contents_class`) — `add`/`remove`/`remove_line_item` (`:22`–`:35`) and `update_cart` (`:37`). Each calls `after_add_or_remove` (`:68`) → `reload_totals` (`:75`) → `order.recalculate`.
2. `advance` (`:50`) drives `order.next` through the state machine.
3. `Spree::OrderMerger#merge!` (`core/app/models/spree/order_merger.rb:48`) folds a guest cart into another, then `persist_merge` → `order.recalculate` (`:140`).
4. State-machine transitions (`core/app/models/spree/core/state_machines/order/class_methods.rb:38`–`118`): `before_transition to: :delivery` runs `create_proposed_shipments` + `ensure_available_shipping_rates` (`:85`–`:87`); the `complete` event chain runs `validate_line_item_availability → ensure_promotions_eligible → ensure_line_item_variants_are_not_deleted → ensure_inventory_units → process_payments_before_complete` then `after_transition to: :complete → finalize` (`:95`–`:103`). A generic `after_transition ... order.recalculate` (`:107`) reruns recalculation on most transitions.

**Recalculation fan-out** (default `Spree::OrderUpdater`, `core/app/models/spree/order_updater.rb`; the in-memory variant `Spree::InMemoryOrderUpdater` mirrors it). `recalculate` (`order_updater.rb:19`) runs **in this order**, inside a transaction:
1. `recalculate_line_item_prices` (`:245`) — re-prices line items if `Spree::Config.recalculate_cart_prices`.
2. `recalculate_item_count` (`:185`).
3. `update_shipment_amounts` (`:141`) → `shipment.update_amounts` on each.
4. `update_totals` (`:134`): `recalculate_payment_total` → `recalculate_item_total` → `recalculate_shipment_total` → `update_adjustment_total`.
5. `update_adjustment_total` (`:169`) → `update_adjustments` (`:114`): `update_promotions` (`:218`, via `Spree::Config.promotions.order_adjuster_class`) → `update_tax_adjustments` (`:222`, via `tax_adjuster_class`) → `recalculate_item_totals` (`:230`, via `item_total_class`), then sums `adjustment_total`/`included_tax_total`/`additional_tax_total` and `recalculate_order_total` (`:163`).
6. If `order.completed?`: `recalculate_payment_state` (`:66`) → `update_shipments` (`:146`, `shipment.update_state`) → `recalculate_shipment_state` (`:47`).
7. `persist_totals` (`:198`, `order.save!`), then `Spree::Bus.publish(:order_recalculated, ...)` (`:32`).

Promotion engines extend this via patches: `legacy_promotions/app/patches/models/solidus_legacy_promotions/spree_order_updater_patch.rb` & `..._in_memory_order_updater_patch.rb`; `promotions/app/patches/models/solidus_promotions/order_updater_patch.rb` & `in_memory_order_updater_patch.rb`.

## Step 5 — How order state/completeness is guarded

The recurring guard is `completed?` (`order.rb:251`, = `completed_at.present?`) / the state machine's `complete?`, plus shipment/payment sub-states. Where they live:

**On the Order itself:**
- `recalculate` only recomputes payment/shipment state `if order.completed?` — `order_updater.rb:25`, `in_memory_order_updater.rb:50`.
- `create_proposed_shipments` raises `CannotRebuildShipments` `if completed?` — `order.rb:505`.
- `check_shipments_and_restart_checkout` mutates only `if !completed? && shipments.all?(&:pending?)` — `order.rb:526`.
- `restart_checkout_flow` returns early unless not yet `cart` — `order.rb:537`.
- `update_cart` skips line-item pruning `unless order.completed?` — `simple_order_contents.rb:39`.
- `allow_cancel?` requires `completed? && state != "canceled"` and a shippable `shipment_state` — `order.rb:297`.
- `can_ship?` = `complete? || resumed? || awaiting_return? || returned?` — `order.rb:407`.
- `email_required?` excludes `cart`/`address` states — `order.rb:792`.
- State machine transition guards — `class_methods.rb:41`–`108`: `cancel` only `from: :complete` w/ `allow_cancel?`; `return` w/ `all_inventory_units_returned?`; `resume` `from: :canceled`; the `complete` chain (`validate_line_item_availability`, `ensure_promotions_eligible`, `ensure_inventory_units`, `process_payments_before_complete`).

**Payments:**
- `Spree::Payment#update_order` recalculates only `if order.completed? || completed? || void?` — `payment.rb:201`.
- `Spree::Payment::Processing#capture!` returns early `if completed?` — `payment/processing.rb:74`.
- `process_payments_with` short-circuits when `payment_total >= total` — `order/payments.rb:42`.

**Shipments:**
- `editable_by?` = `!shipped?` — `shipment.rb:111`.
- `ensure_can_destroy` aborts destroy if `shipped? || canceled?` — `shipment.rb:366`.

**Stock / cancellations:**
- `Spree::OrderCancellations` wraps every mutation in `Spree::OrderMutex.with_lock!(@order)` — `order_cancellations.rb:49`,`:77`,`:99`; `short_ship` asserts all units belong to the order (`:43`). The mutex itself (`order_mutex.rb:15`) enforces single-writer via a unique row.

**Promotions / adjustments:**
- `SolidusPromotions::Benefit#raise_for_adjustments_for_completed_orders` aborts destroy if any adjustment joins a `Spree::Order.complete` — `benefit.rb:341`.
- `Spree::AdjustmentSource#remove_adjustments_from_incomplete_orders` scopes deletion to `Spree::Order.incomplete` — `adjustment_source.rb:5`.
- Scopes backing these guards: `Order.complete`/`incomplete`/`canceled`/`not_canceled` — `order.rb:205`–`219`.

## Step 6 — Blast radius, ranked by risk

`sense_blast` verdict: **high risk, 135 direct callers, 310 affected symbols across 53 files, partial resolution.** Ranked:

**Tier 1 — most likely to break (recalc/teardown internals):**
1. `Spree::OrderUpdater` + `Spree::InMemoryOrderUpdater` — `order_updater.rb`, `in_memory_order_updater.rb`. They reach directly into `line_items`/`shipments`/`adjustments`/`payments` and the `completed?` branch; any change to storage or recalc order breaks totals/states.
2. ---

# Order Contract Audit — Solidus

## Step 1 — Orientation

**What it is:** Solidus, a full-stack e-commerce framework for Rails, split into cooperating gems (each a Rails engine), all namespaced under `Spree::` (and the newer `SolidusAdmin::` / `SolidusPromotions::`).

**Gem layout** (repo root):
- `core/` — domain models, services, state machines, config (`Spree::Config`). The heart.
- `api/` — JSON API controllers (`Spree::Api::*`).
- `backend/` — legacy admin UI (`Spree::Admin::*`) + `Spree::BackendConfiguration`.
- `admin/` — new admin UI built on ViewComponents (`SolidusAdmin::*`).
- `promotions/` — current promotions system (`SolidusPromotions::*`).
- `legacy_promotions/` — deprecated promotions (`Spree::Promotion::*`), applied as patches.
- `sample/`, `lib/`, `tasks/` — fixtures, meta-gem, rake tasks.

**The central model:** `Spree::Order` — `core/app/models/spree/order.rb:21`. Doc comment (`:4`): "The customers cart until completed, then acts as permanent record of the transaction." Everything hangs off it: line items, shipments, inventory units, payments, adjustments, returns, promotions.

**Shared foundation it's built on:**
- `Spree::Base` — `core/app/models/spree/base.rb:3` (abstract AR base; includes `Spree::Core::Permalinks`, `Spree::RansackableAttributes`).
- `Spree::Config` — the configuration registry that swaps in every collaborator class (recalculator, contents, shipping, cancellations, merger, stock coordinator, promotions adjuster). The Order delegates almost all behavior through it.

---

## Step 2 — The Order contract and what it pulls in

**Model:** `core/app/models/spree/order.rb:21`.

**Concerns / mixins included (`:26`–`:42`):**
- `::Spree::Config.state_machines.order` (`:26`) → `Spree::Core::StateMachines::Order` at `core/app/models/spree/core/state_machines/order.rb:6`, which on include extends `ClassMethods` (`core/app/models/spree/core/state_machines/order/class_methods.rb:7`) and includes `Spree::StateChangeTracking` (`core/app/models/concerns/spree/state_change_tracking.rb:4` — `after_update` enqueues a state-change job).
- `Spree::Order::Payments` (`:28`) → `core/app/models/spree/order/payments.rb:5` (`process_payments!`, `authorize_payments!`, `capture_payments!`, `unprocessed_payments`; return value drives the `complete` transition).
- `Metadata` (`:29`) → `core/app/models/concerns/spree/metadata.rb:4` (adds `customer_metadata`/`admin_metadata` JSON + validation).
- `extend Spree::DisplayMoney` + `money_methods(...)` (`:42`–`:59`) → generates `display_*` for all the money totals.

**Associations other records hang off it (`:78`–`:131`):**
- Customer: `user`, `bill_address`, `ship_address`, `store`, `created_by`, `approver`, `canceler`.
- Items: `line_items` (`:93`, `dependent: :destroy`), `variants`, `products`.
- Shipping: `shipments` (`:98`, `dependent: :destroy`), `inventory_units` (through shipments), `cartons`.
- Adjustments/promos: `adjustments` (`:107`, polymorphic `adjustable`, autosave), `line_item_adjustments`, `shipment_adjustments`, `all_adjustments` (`:110`, `dependent: :destroy`, by `order_id`).
- Payments: `payments` (`:117`, `dependent: :destroy`), `valid_store_credit_payments`.
- Returns: `return_authorizations` (`:121`), `return_items`, `customer_returns`, `reimbursements`, `refunds`.
- `state_changes` (`:128`, polymorphic `stateful`).
- `accepts_nested_attributes_for` line_items/addresses/payments/shipments (`:133`–`:137`).

**Key contract surface a teammate must know:**

- **Service accessors:** `contents` (`:310`), `shipping` (`:314`), `cancellations` (`:318`), `merge!` (`:457`) — each instantiates a `Spree::Config.*_class`.

- **Event bus:** publishes `:order_emptied`, `:order_finalized`, `:order_canceled`, and (via recalculator) `:order_recalculated` — external subscribers depend on these.

## Step 3 — How an order is built and recalculated

**Build / mutate (cart → order):**
1. `Spree::SimpleOrderContents` (`core/app/models/spree/simple_order_contents.rb:4`, the `order_contents_class`) — `add`/`remove`/`remove_line_item` (`:22`–`:35`) and `update_cart` (`:37`). Each calls `after_add_or_remove` (`:68`) → `reload_totals` (`:75`) → `order.recalculate`.
2. `advance` (`:50`) drives `order.next` through the state machine.
3. `Spree::OrderMerger#merge!` (`core/app/models/spree/order_merger.rb:48`) folds a guest cart into another, then `persist_merge` → `order.recalculate` (`:140`).
4. State-machine transitions (`core/app/models/spree/core/state_machines/order/class_methods.rb:38`–`118`): `before_transition to: :delivery` runs `create_proposed_shipments` + `ensure_available_shipping_rates` (`:85`–`:87`); the `complete` event chain runs `validate_line_item_availability → ensure_promotions_eligible → ensure_line_item_variants_are_not_deleted → ensure_inventory_units → process_payments_before_complete` then `after_transition to: :complete → finalize` (`:95`–`:103`). A generic `after_transition ... order.recalculate` (`:107`) reruns recalculation on most transitions.

**Recalculation fan-out** (default `Spree::OrderUpdater`, `core/app/models/spree/order_updater.rb`; the in-memory variant `Spree::InMemoryOrderUpdater` mirrors it). `recalculate` (`order_updater.rb:19`) runs **in this order**, inside a transaction:
1. `recalculate_line_item_prices` (`:245`) — re-prices line items if `Spree::Config.recalculate_cart_prices`.
2. `recalculate_item_count` (`:185`).
3. `update_shipment_amounts` (`:141`) → `shipment.update_amounts` on each.
4. `update_totals` (`:134`): `recalculate_payment_total` → `recalculate_item_total` → `recalculate_shipment_total` → `update_adjustment_total`.
5. `update_adjustment_total` (`:169`) → `update_adjustments` (`:114`): `update_promotions` (`:218`, via `Spree::Config.promotions.order_adjuster_class`) → `update_tax_adjustments` (`:222`, via `tax_adjuster_class`) → `recalculate_item_totals` (`:230`, via `item_total_class`), then sums `adjustment_total`/`included_tax_total`/`additional_tax_total` and `recalculate_order_total` (`:163`).
6. If `order.completed?`: `recalculate_payment_state` (`:66`) → `update_shipments` (`:146`, `shipment.update_state`) → `recalculate_shipment_state` (`:47`).
7. `persist_totals` (`:198`, `order.save!`), then `Spree::Bus.publish(:order_recalculated, ...)` (`:32`).

Promotion engines extend this via patches: `legacy_promotions/app/patches/models/solidus_legacy_promotions/spree_order_updater_patch.rb` & `..._in_memory_order_updater_patch.rb`; `promotions/app/patches/models/solidus_promotions/order_updater_patch.rb` & `in_memory_order_updater_patch.rb`.

## Step 5 — How order state/completeness is guarded

The recurring guard is `completed?` (`order.rb:251`, = `completed_at.present?`) / the state machine's `complete?`, plus shipment/payment sub-states. Where they live:

**On the Order itself:**
- `recalculate` only recomputes payment/shipment state `if order.completed?` — `order_updater.rb:25`, `in_memory_order_updater.rb:50`.
- `create_proposed_shipments` raises `CannotRebuildShipments` `if completed?` — `order.rb:505`.
- `check_shipments_and_restart_checkout` mutates only `if !completed? && shipments.all?(&:pending?)` — `order.rb:526`.
- `restart_checkout_flow` returns early unless not yet `cart` — `order.rb:537`.
- `update_cart` skips line-item pruning `unless order.completed?` — `simple_order_contents.rb:39`.
- `allow_cancel?` requires `completed? && state != "canceled"` and a shippable `shipment_state` — `order.rb:297`.
- `can_ship?` = `complete? || resumed? || awaiting_return? || returned?` — `order.rb:407`.
- `email_required?` excludes `cart`/`address` states — `order.rb:792`.
- State machine transition guards — `class_methods.rb:41`–`108`: `cancel` only `from: :complete` w/ `allow_cancel?`; `return` w/ `all_inventory_units_returned?`; `resume` `from: :canceled`; the `complete` chain (`validate_line_item_availability`, `ensure_promotions_eligible`, `ensure_inventory_units`, `process_payments_before_complete`).

**Payments:**
- `Spree::Payment#update_order` recalculates only `if order.completed? || completed? || void?` — `payment.rb:201`.
- `Spree::Payment::Processing#capture!` returns early `if completed?` — `payment/processing.rb:74`.
- `process_payments_with` short-circuits when `payment_total >= total` — `order/payments.rb:42`.

**Shipments:**
- `editable_by?` = `!shipped?` — `shipment.rb:111`.
- `ensure_can_destroy` aborts destroy if `shipped? || canceled?` — `shipment.rb:366`.

**Stock / cancellations:**
- `Spree::OrderCancellations` wraps every mutation in `Spree::OrderMutex.with_lock!(@order)` — `order_cancellations.rb:49`,`:77`,`:99`; `short_ship` asserts all units belong to the order (`:43`). The mutex itself (`order_mutex.rb:15`) enforces single-writer via a unique row.

**Promotions / adjustments:**
- `SolidusPromotions::Benefit#raise_for_adjustments_for_completed_orders` aborts destroy if any adjustment joins a `Spree::Order.complete` — `benefit.rb:341`.
- `Spree::AdjustmentSource#remove_adjustments_from_incomplete_orders` scopes deletion to `Spree::Order.incomplete` — `adjustment_source.rb:5`.
- Scopes backing these guards: `Order.complete`/`incomplete`/`canceled`/`not_canceled` — `order.rb:205`–`219`.

## Step 6 — Blast radius, ranked by risk

`sense_blast` verdict: **high risk, 135 direct callers, 310 affected symbols across 53 files, partial resolution.** Ranked:

**Tier 1 — most likely to break (recalc/teardown internals):**
1. `Spree::OrderUpdater` + `Spree::InMemoryOrderUpdater` — `order_updater.rb`, `in_memory_order_updater.rb`. They reach directly into `line_items`/`shipments`/`adjustments`/`payments` and the `completed?` branch; any change to storage or recalc order breaks totals/states.
2. The four promotion **patches** to those updaters (`legacy_promotions/.../spree_order_updater_patch.rb`, `..._in_memory_order_updater_patch.rb`; `promotions/.../order_updater_patch.rb`, `in_memory_order_updater_patch.rb`) — they `prepend`/patch recalc internals and will silently drift if method names/sequence change.
3. `Spree::Order#empty!`, `after_cancel`, `finalize`, `create_proposed_shipments`, `check_shipments_and_restart_checkout` — `order.rb:461`,`846`,`758`,`504`,`525`. These ARE the teardown contract being reworked.
4. Event-bus subscribers of `:order_recalculated` / `:order_emptied` / `:order_canceled` / `:order_finalized` (published at `order_updater.rb:32`, `order.rb:465`,`853`,`774`) — invisible to static callers; a renamed/dropped event silently no-ops downstream.

**Tier 2 — high risk (direct association mutation):**
5. `Spree::Shipment` (`shipment.rb:9`,`111`,`204`,`223`,`366`), `Spree::Payment` (`payment.rb:16`,`201`), `Spree::Adjustment` (`adjustment.rb:12`), `Spree::LineItem` (`line_item.rb:15`) — all `touch: true`/recalc-on-change; sensitive to `dependent: :destroy` and autosave changes.
6. `Spree::OrderCancellations` / `Spree::OrderShipping` / `Spree::OrderMutex` — `order_cancellations.rb`, `order_shipping.rb`, `order_mutex.rb`. Teardown of inventory + locking; reorder of destroy vs recalc can corrupt state.
7. `Spree::Stock::SimpleCoordinator` / `Stock::Package` — `simple_coordinator.rb:84`, `package.rb:22`. Rebuild shipments from order; affected by shipment teardown changes.
8. `Spree::SimpleOrderContents` / `Spree::OrderMerger` — `simple_order_contents.rb`, `order_merger.rb`. Cart build + recalc.

**Tier 3 — medium (derive from order, guard on state):**
9. Promotions: `SolidusPromotions::Benefit:341`, `AdjustmentSource:5`, legacy `Promotion#discounted_orders`/rules, the `order_adjuster_class` invoked inside recalc — depend on `complete`/`incomplete` scopes surviving.
10. Returns/exchange: `Spree::Exchange:23`, `Reimbursement`, `ReturnAuthorization`, `CustomerReturn`.

**Tier 4 — lower (surface, mostly read/params):**
11. Controllers/components in `api/`, `backend/`, `admin/` and `PermissionSets` — break loudly (specs/CI) rather than silently.

**Must be re-verified after the change:** totals (`item_total`/`shipment_total`/`adjustment_total`/`tax`/`total`), `payment_state` & `shipment_state` derivation, the `complete`/`cancel`/`resume`/`return` transitions, `empty!`/teardown + `dependent: :destroy` cascades, OrderMutex locking, promotion recalc through both engines, and every published Bus event.
